jobs.description:
We are seeking an experienced Operations Planning Leader to drive strategic network forecasting and inventory optimization in our Special Fulfillment team, with a focus on Express Mart and Amazon Now services. This role will be based at Tokyo and will be instrumental in transforming our delivery logistics through innovative planning strategies and data-driven decision-making.

In this position, you will lead the development of comprehensive inbound plans from Distribution Centers (DC) or Fulfillment Centers (FC) to Express Mart or Amazon Now sites, optimizing resource allocation across our expanding delivery network. You'll work at the intersection of strategic planning and operational excellence, creating forecasting models that balance fullness targets, cost efficiency and other metrics.

As a key member of our Supply Chain Management team, you'll collaborate with cross-functional partners including Business, Supply Chain Optimization Technologies (SCOT), Transport Services, and Finance to align operational strategies across Japan.

This role requires a strategic thinker who can independently lead complex planning operations, translate data insights into actionable plans, and maintain flexibility to adapt strategies based on evolving business needs. You'll be responsible for driving accuracy in inbound planning (Plan vs. Actual variance minimization), maintaining optimal inventory levels, and ensuring cost adherence to operational plans—all while supporting the next generation of customer delivery experiences.

If you thrive in fast-paced, innovative environments and are passionate about revolutionizing delivery networks through analytical rigor and collaborative problem-solving, this is your opportunity to make a significant impact on Japan's logistics landscape.

Key job responsibilities
Strategic Planning & Forecasting
- Develop 13-week rolling inbound plans from DC/FC for Express Mart and Amazon Now operations across Japan
- Create network forecasts optimizing inventory placement, Fullness target, cost efficiency and other metrics
- Lead S&OP processes for Express Mart and Amazon Now operations, aligning demand forecasts with supply capabilities

Operational Excellence
- Drive Plan vs. Actual variance minimization through continuous monitoring and adjustment
- Analyze operational metrics including site fullness, inventory health (DoC, WoC), transfer efficiency, and cost adherence
- Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify root causes and develop data-driven solutions

Cross-Functional Collaboration
- Partner with Business, SCOT, AMZL, and Finance teams to align planning strategies

Network Expansion & Innovation
- Support regional network expansion and new site launches
- Lead planning initiatives for emerging services

Process Improvement
- Identify and implement process improvements enhancing planning accuracy and operational efficiency
- Lead complex cross-functional projects with accountability for deliverables and timelines

About the team
We are the Special Fulfillment team in Supply Chain Management driving innovation in Japan's delivery logistics. Based at Tokyo, our collaborative group focuses on optimizing Express Mart, Amazon Now and Cold Chain operations across Japan. We partner closely with Business, SCOT, AMZL and Finance to revolutionize network planning and inventory optimization.

Our team thrives on data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement, balancing operational efficiency with customer experience. We're committed to expanding regional delivery capabilities, including emerging cold chain logistics, while maintaining the highest standards of planning accuracy and cost efficiency. Join us in shaping the future of Japan's fulfillment network.

- Experience in traditional and emerging media channels including TV, OOH, cinema, digital display, online video and paid social

jobs.description:
At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners. We are dreamers and inventors who come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences to empower and inspire each other. Imagine your future with us.

ABOUT THIS ROLE
The Audible Books Content Acquisition Team EU owns the full cycle business relationships with the authors, writers, content creators, literary agents and recognized IP brands that serve as Audible’s top global creators in the literary space. In this role you’ll generate, negotiate, and close a high volume of new deals and deal renewals for audio original and book-based audio content with prospective and current top partners, at terms that benefit Audible customers. You’ll have responsibility for acquired content, ensure successful post-signing go to market planning across the relevant teams at Audible and Amazon, and contribute to defining strategic development affecting Audible’s overall growth and success.

As the Manager, Audible Books Content Acquisition EU, you will...
- Identify, initiate and develop relationships with new and existing content partners across EU territories, fostering long-term collaboration and trust with the broader rights holder and creative community
- Independently manage a high-volume deal flow end-to-end, from structuring and negotiating to executing agreements, ensuring all deals are aligned with Audible's content strategy and initiatives
- Identify and prepare projects for internal support and valuation by presenting data, preparing budgets, P&Ls, and drafting proposed deal terms
- Work cross-functionally with various internal stakeholders including Editorial/Creative, Finance, BI, Marketing, PR, Content Services, Content Partnerships, and Content Creation teams
- Track and communicate deal terms within the organization and provide for first line of enforcement of deal terms if necessary
- Coordinate closely with members of the content teams at Audible to maintain consistency in messaging and policy
- Build and manage the deal architecture, negotiations and day-to-day deal requirements for Audible Produced EU content
- Develop and improve the quality and efficiency of all our EU deal terms, negotiations, and agreements to support our direct relations to content creators
- Work hand-in-hand with our European Legal and Business Affairs teams on complex rights and contracts negotiation
- Collaborate with the global cross functional teams to develop deal strategy, formulate key terms including all related analysis, strategizing and planning for a variety of complex agreements, and negotiations with local or global creators

ABOUT AUDIBLE
Audible is the leading producer and provider of audio storytelling. We spark listeners’ imaginations, offering immersive, cinematic experiences full of inspiration and insight to enrich our customers daily lives. We are a global company with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are dreamers and inventors who are passionate about the positive impact Audible can make for our customers and our neighbors. This spirit courses throughout Audible, supporting a culture of creativity and inclusion built on our People Principles and our mission to build more equitable communities in the cities we call home.
jobs.description_short: At Audible, we believe stories have the power to transform lives. It’s why we work with some of the world’s leading creators to produce and share audio storytelling with our millions of global listeners.
jobs.preferred_qualifications:
- Speak, write, and read fluently in French, Spanish, German or Italian
- LLB (Bachelor of Laws) or LLM (Masters of Laws), preferably in Intellectual Property and/or Media Law
- Proven and specialised experience in the audio rights space, with hands-on involvement in acquiring or selling audio rights within the publishing industry
- Proven experience in building and nurturing professional relationships with creative individuals, communities, and industry organisations.
- Passionate about storytelling, the evolution of audio, theater, music, books, and consuming audio content
- Resourceful self-starter with an independent attitude and focus on excellence

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

The Critical Projects Implementation (CPI) team is a project management and execution team that manages construction activity within the operational data center spaces. The CPI team is tasked with critical infrastructure improvement projects to optimize utilization of space, power, and cooling within operation data centers around the globe. These activities are outside of the scope of standard construction delivery and regular preventative maintenance tasks.

We are currently seeking a Data Center Project Manager to serve as a technical resource within Amazon data centers. You will be part of a highly creative, efficient team tasked with tackling the most fascinating and challenges in designing, building, and operating Amazon data facilities. The Project Manager is ultimately responsible for project oversight and review of all disciplines including electrical, mechanical, controls, and architectural. A fundamental understanding of these systems is required, as the individual will be expected to identify areas for improvement as well as act on data provided from other team and organizations.

Our DC Project Managers design, develop and deploy innovative solutions to address operational challenges. They work independently as well as the ability to work within multi-disciplinary teams. The scope of projects will include but not limited to the improvement of electrical, mechanical, fire detection and building automation system. The Project Manager is responsible for the overall direction, coordination, implementation, execution, control and completion of specific projects ensuring consistency with company strategy, commitments and goals.

If you are passionate about the Customer Experience; you think big; and you want to contribute to the operational excellence of Amazon data centers then this may be the challenge you’ve been looking for!

Key job responsibilities
Project management and oversight of construction related activities as they relate to modifying existing infrastructure for the purpose of installing new or to replace end of life equipment. This will include the ownership of the project scope, timeline, and budget.
- Provide project management for specific initiatives aimed at increasing the resiliency of our data centers.
- Electrical design and review of designs associated with the optimization of existing data centers.
- Construction/project package drawing review.
- Creation of project scope, request for proposals, and capital requests.
- Total project quality including the creation of a commissioning plan and oversight of the execution.
- Recording and reporting key metrics to team members and management.
- Operational support including review of procedures, best practices, and maintenance initiatives.
- Drive teams to perform troubleshooting and root cause failure analysis associated with equipment or electrical failures.
- Work with contractors and vendors to deliver efficient and high quality project turnovers.
- Be a leader within the group as well as within internal and external teams that support the data center.


A day in the life
You will be part of a highly creative, efficient team tasked with tackling the most fascinating and challenges in designing, building, and operating Amazon data facilities. The Project Manager is ultimately responsible for project oversight and review of all disciplines including electrical, mechanical, controls, and architectural. A fundamental understanding of these systems is required, you will be expected to identify areas for improvement as well as act on data provided from other team and organizations. Ideal candidates will possess the ability to design, develop and deploy innovative solutions to address operational challenges. This role requires both independent contribution as well as the ability to work within multi-disciplinary teams. The scope of projects will include but not limited to the improvement of electrical, mechanical, fire detection and building automation system. The Project Manager is responsible for the overall direction, coordination, implementation, execution, control and completion of specific projects ensuring consistency with company strategy, commitments and goals.

If you are passionate about the Customer Experience; you think big; and you want to contribute to the operational excellence of Amazon data centers then this may be the opportunity you’ve been looking for!

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

jobs.description:
“WARNING: Choking Hazard — small parts. Not for children under 3 years.”

“WARNING: This product can expose you to chemicals including antimony trioxide, which are known to cause cancer or birth defects or other reproductive harm.”

Have you ever seen these warning labels on popular and seemingly innocent products, such as fidget spinners or food containers? As the Buyer Safety Experience team for Amazon Retail worldwide, we aim to raise the bar when it comes to educating and empowering consumers about product safety. Our team is responsible for the complete end-to-end safety experience for buyers — from the warnings you see on the product detail pages (and how to turn them into useful, actionable information), to making safer product recommendations targeted to each individual consumer’s needs, to informing consumers of recalled products and preventing them from being delivered to their doorstep.

As the founding engineer of the Buyer Safety Experience - Romania team, you’ll be able to build the groundwork and influence its direction for the years to come. Our work cuts across various disciplines — from delivering an awesome customer and brand experience via great UI/UX, to building massively scalable distributed systems to support the most high-traffic pages on Amazon worldwide, to analytical and feedback systems which give us data-driven customer insights, to using GenAI to enable Selling Partners and brands to keep customers safe and informed. If you have a passion for both consumer and business-facing applications, and are obsessed with customer experience, we want you!

Key job responsibilities

As the first Software Development Engineer of the Buyer Safety Experience team in Romania, you will hire and lead a brand new team, and work closely with cross-functional stakeholders worldwide across Program and Product. Your role will be heavily customer-obsessed, building solutions to ensure Amazon is Earth's safest place to shop.

A day in the life
- Hiring: Build a new team from scratch
- Deeply understand technical decisions, influence long-term architecture
- Define the team's yearly goals and long-term technical vision and charter, along with product and business stakeholders
- Work closely with international stakeholders across our VP-level Trustworthy Shopping Experience org, in addition to TPM and PM-T

Key job responsibilities
As the first Software Development Engineer of the Buyer Safety Experience team in RO, you will hire and lead a new team, and work closely with cross-functional stakeholders worldwide across Program and Product. Your role will be heavily customer-obsessed, building solutions to ensure Amazon is Earth's safest place to shop.

jobs.description:
Amazon は、Amazon の物流拠点 (カスタマーフルフィルメント、Amazon Transportation Services、Amazon ロジスティクス、またはグローバルスペシャリティフルフィルメント) の大量採用プログラムをリードする意欲のあるスタッフィングリード (リクルーター/調達) を募集しています。
スタッフィングリードは、物流拠点での円滑な入出荷を実現するための人員確保をリードします。
主要なビジネス パートナーとともに Workforce Staffing (WFS) のオペレーショナル エクセレンスを推進するために、高いモチベーションと柔軟性を持っています。
成功する候補者は、優れたコンサルティングスキル、優れた影響力、組織の専門知識、口頭および書面による優れたコミュニケーションスキル、事業運営を改善するデータ主導型プログラムの構築への情熱を示す必要があります。
また多少の不確実さがあっても柔軟に受け入れることができ、最高クラスのプログラムを開発する上では、革新的であることが求められます。
・採用計画の立案や募集（求人広告等の出稿）を企画・実行するチームに対して最適な情報をインプットします。
・物流拠点運営責任者との定期的な会議を通じて、人員確保の進捗を共有します。問題が発生した場合は、チームで協力して解決にあたります。
・スタッフィングリードは、派遣会社との交渉を通じて、必要な人員確保を促進するとともに、Amazonで直接採用をした人材の充足の役割を担います。
·隣接する物流拠点を複数担当していただきます。物流拠点での採用イベント・内定者イベント等をリードしますので、日帰り・宿泊の出張が発生します。（現在は原則オンラインで実施しています）
·業務はマイクロソフトオフィスを使用します。特にエクセルとワードは日常的に使用します。
エクセルは、ピボットテーブル、IF関数、VLOOKUP関数等を使用してデータ分析を行います。
また、報告や提案など様々な場面において、ワードで文書を作成しています。
· 問題解決と分析に厳密なアプローチを取り、データから結論を導き出すとともに、ステークホルダーやオペレーションチームに、専門知識と技術的もしくは実行可能なガイダンスを提供します。
· ボトルネックの予測、エスカレーションの管理、トレードオフの予測と判断、ビジネスニーズと制約のバランスを取ります。
·ビジネスの優先順位を確立し、ステークホルダーと協力してリスクと緩和策を特定、伝達し、目標、マイルストーン、完了日を策定、維持します。
·上級レベルのステークホルダーにアイデアを提示し、フィードバックを受け入れ、変更をすばやく適用します。
·機密情報や重要な情報をすぐに判断して扱う能力
·実務スキルと知的スキルを兼ね備え、また、複雑な分析、実践的なプロジェクト、プロジェクト管理などの様々な業務を迅速にこなし、適応できる能力
·
業務上、英語を使用する機会があります。海外のチームとの定期的な会議への参加やレポート作成、メール等で使用する機会があります。また、海外のチームとの連携は業務遂行上とても重要です。

主な責任には以下が含まれますが、これらに限定されません。
· 採用プロセスにおける潜在的な問題を特定し、是正措置/エスカレーションが完了していることを確認する
· オペレーション指標を深く掘り下げて、パフォーマンスの向上、不具合のトラブルシューティング、目標達成のための是正措置の策定を行う。
· オペレーション計画に携わるビジネスアナリストと直接対話し、指標の方法論と検証に関する議論を行う 。
· 顧客/パートナーのニーズとタスク分析を行い、プロジェクトの範囲を定め、既存のプロセスを評価してソリューションを提案する。

（キャリアパス）
複数のスタッフィングリードをまとめるマネージャーが最も身近なキャリアパスです。
Amazonでは自分のキャリアは自分で開発するという文化が根付いています。社内公募制も年間を通じて運用されており、様々なキャリアのチャンスが提供されています。

Amazon is looking for staffing leads (recruiters) that are motivated to lead mass recruitment programs for Amazon's logistics sites (Customer Fulfillment, Amazon Transportation Services, Amazon Logistics, or Global Specialty Fulfillment). Staffing Leads take the lead in securing personnel to achieve smooth receipt and shipment at logistics hubs. They are highly motivated and flexible to drive operational excellence for Workforce Staffing (WFS) with its major business partners.

A successful candidate must demonstrate strong consulting skills, exceptional ability to influence, organizational expertise, strong verbal and written communication skills, and a passion for building data driven programs that improve business operations. They are self-motivated, solutions-oriented, comfortable with ambiguity, and innovative in developing best-in-class programs.

Key Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
Strategic Workforce Planning & Execution
• Shape the future of workforce strategy by developing and executing comprehensive staffing plans aligned with business objectives, validating Labor Orders (Hiring Requirements) per guardrails, and participating in capacity planning calls
• Drive data-driven decision making by analyzing hiring trends, attrition patterns, and workforce metrics to uncover opportunities, deep dive operational performance to troubleshoot defects, and create remedial action plans that consistently deliver results
• Lead continuous improvement initiatives that optimize hiring efficiency, quality, and speed-to-fill while reducing cost-per-hire through systematic process enhancements and automation
• Partner strategically with external staffing vendor managers on site-specific hiring and conversion strategies for business-critical locations, aligning on execution plans
• Navigate complexity with agility by anticipating bottlenecks, providing escalation management, making strategic tradeoffs, and balancing competing business needs versus constraints
Daily Operational Leadership & Governance
• Lead daily operational excellence through site-specific huddles focused on capacity optimization and labor hour management, while facilitating Staffing Lead and Staffing Associate daily standup meetings for issue resolution and strategic alignment
• Serve as the go-to expert for day-to-day staffing inquiries from Operations teams and vendor partners, proactively identifying and resolving potential hiring process issues through systematic monitoring
• Engage in strategic governance through active participation in RLC and escalation reviews, ensuring corrective actions are completed timely
• Build executive relationships through regular 1-1 meetings with senior leadership for strategic guidance, alignment, and decision-making support
• Establish clear priorities while identifying risks early, developing mitigation plans, and communicating business priorities in close collaboration with stakeholders
Team Leadership & Development
• Build and inspire high-performing teams by leading, mentoring, and developing Staffing Associates across multiple sites through regular 1-1 meetings, meaningful performance reviews, and personalized career development discussions
• Drive accountability and results by establishing clear team goals, tracking performance metrics, holding team accountable for outcomes, and celebrating achievements
• Develop future leaders by identifying training needs and creating capability-building programs that elevate team performance and prepare associates for advancement
• Cultivate a winning culture rooted in customer obsession, ownership, innovation, and continuous improvement across all team interactions and initiatives
Blue Badge (Direct) Hiring Leadership
• Champion direct hiring initiatives by leading referral events, HelloWork partnerships, conversion programs, and in-person New Hire Events (NHE) at sites to create memorable candidate experiences
• Connect with the frontline and community through regular Gemba walks to generate referrals and gather associate feedback, while visiting HelloWork offices to strengthen partnerships and expand candidate sourcing
• Drive innovative sourcing strategies by collaborating with Operations, Agencies, and Marketing teams to identify conversion candidates and optimize both paid and non-paid candidate attraction channels
• Expand diverse talent pipelines through specialized channels including foreign workers, PWD (Persons with Disabilities), and DEI initiatives, while forging community connections through partnerships with vocational schools and community organizations
Green Badge (Contractual) Workforce Management
• Orchestrate vendor coordination and regional alignment through weekly meetings to confirm Labor Order allocation, facilitate Operations and UTR team updates, and ensure operational readiness
• Execute Path to Green (PTG) excellence by onboarding new agencies, strengthening existing partnerships, optimizing shift allocation strategies, and managing vendor relationships effectively
• Ensure system accuracy and operational excellence by resolving Octomate staff rostering errors, handling Beeline system issues, maintaining data integrity, and monitoring Green Badge rostering accuracy
• Optimize workforce utilization by tracking attendance patterns, productivity metrics, and workforce performance while driving retention initiatives that reduce early attrition and enhance associate engagement
Stakeholder Management & External Partnerships
• Serve as a strategic business partner to Site Operations leadership on all workforce planning matters, conducting regular site-agency visits for coordination and partnership development
• Influence senior leadership by presenting compelling workforce insights, hiring forecasts, and strategic recommendations while collaborating with Business Analysts on operational planning and metric methodology
• Build cross-functional excellence by partnering with HR, Finance, Learning & Development, and Safety teams to deliver integrated workforce solutions
• Ensure compliance and integrity with labor laws, agency contracts, and Amazon policies across all operations while handling sensitive matters professionally with appropriate discretion and confidentiality
Systems & Data Management
• Own data integrity and system excellence by ensuring accuracy in HRMS systems for all associates, overseeing proactive maintenance, and driving rapid error resolution for workforce management tools
• Transform data into insights by leveraging analytics to generate actionable intelligence, inform strategic decisions, and perform customer/partner needs analysis
• Champion automation and innovation by implementing process improvements, evaluating existing processes, scoping projects, and proposing solutions that enhance operational efficiency

A day in the life
The WFS Team has an environment where team members collaborate, support each other, and learn together. As a Staffing Lead, you will balance strategic planning with hands-on execution, conducting daily huddles with operations, leading your team through coaching and development, executing hiring events on the floor, managing vendor relationships, and presenting workforce insights. You will travel to multiple assigned sites for hiring events and stakeholder meetings (day trips and overnight stays may be required). You will use Microsoft Office applications daily, particularly Excel and Word. You will work with global teams in English through meetings, reports, and emails.

About the team
The Workforce Staffing (WFS) department, part of a global team, is seeking a teammate who can think from a customer-centric perspective and create further innovation in WFS as a Staffing Lead. This role primarily supports different operations divisions across Japan including Customer Fulfillment, Amazon Transportation Services, Amazon Logistics, and Global Specialty Fulfillment.
jobs.description_short: Amazon は、Amazon の物流拠点 (カスタマーフルフィルメント、Amazon Transportation Services、Amazon ロジスティクス、またはグローバルスペシャリティフルフィルメント) の大量採用プログラムをリードする意欲のあるスタッフィングリード (リクルーター/調達) を募集しています。スタッフィングリードは、物流拠点での円滑な入出荷を実現するための人員確保をリードします。主要なビジネス

- Master's degree in Human Resources, Human Resources Management, Labor Relations, Business/Management, Psychology, or a related field
- 7+ years of HR experience
- Experience in transportation or supply chain within a high-volume logistics, manufacturing or engineering environment
- Experience managing and negotiating contracts and vendor relationships

jobs.description:
We are looking for seasoned Sales Associate, responsible for the identification, onboarding and growth of High Value seller(HVS) accounts on the Amazon.in marketplace. An ideal candidate comes from Brand and Distributor account management background, can thrive in new ambiguous environments where they can develop businesses from scratch, implement account level strategies and iterate growth plans YoY to deliver success for both – HVS Sellers and Amazon. The candidate should be hands-on in managing B2B conversations, detail oriented to present short-term & long-term action plans to CXO layers, possesses ability to deliver positive experience for clients, have relentlessly high standards and can operate as an independent business owner who understands key levers to drive across cross-functional teams for achieving desired results

Key job responsibilities
Business Development: Contribute to business strategy development, identify the target High Value Seller accounts to pitch, align relevant stakeholders for marketplace sales strategy, and onboard the account for selling on Amazon. • Business Growth: Drive complex set of input and outputs metrics that deliver quicker growth, improve the End-customer & Seller experience, and enable scalability of business, in collaboration with cross-functional teams • Relationship Management: Build and cultivate strong relationships with High Value Seller (HVS- Trade Mark Registered Brands and Distributors of National Brands) in your portfolio along with internal stakeholders, acting as a team-player, trusted advisor and a business advocate. • Process Excellence: Use customer feedbacks, market growth trends, and analyze key metrics to contribute to development of features and programs that accelerate HVS account’s growth and improve their collaboration with Amazon. Spot areas of inefficiencies and work to simplify


A day in the life
On a day to day basis, the candidate will be responsible for achieving the revenue for the business by driving strategic conversations, work backwards from account acquisition goals and implement growth strategy for the named HVS accounts. The candidate will execute account level KPIs/metrics by focusing on marketplace brand presence, leveraging available channels for faster delivery to Amazon customers, developing Advertisement strategy and improving selection quality that can generate sales orders. The candidate will use his category expertise to accurately forecast business for the assigned set of accounts, drive HVS engagements and act as internal Voice-of-Seller to influence Marketplace policies and products in areas like Tech Integrations, GTM channels for online, Legal documentations, Payments, Reimbursements etc. The candidate would need to actively collaborate with internal stakeholders like Category, Fulfilment, Finance, Product & Advertising teams to align account level initiatives and similarly external stakeholders at HVS end from Marketing, Commercials, Legal, IT, Supply chain etc. to get sign-offs.

About the team
This role would be part of Direct Sales team, responsible for onboarding of brand and distributors as direct sellers onto Amazon marketplace.

- Experience with SQL
jobs.description:
We are seeking a highly skilled Data Engineer to join our FinTech ADA team, responsible for building and optimizing scalable data pipelines and platforms that power analytics, automation, and decision-making across Finance and Accounting domains. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in AWS cloud technologies including Redshift, S3, AWS Glue, EMR, Kinesis, Firehose, Lambda, and IAM, along with hands-on experience designing secure, efficient, and resilient data architectures.

You will work with large-scale structured and unstructured datasets, leveraging both relational and non-relational data stores (object storage, key-value/document databases, graph, and column-family stores) to deliver reliable, high-performance data solutions. This role requires strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business needs into technical data solutions.

Key job responsibilities
Scope -
Fintech is seeking a Data Engineer to be part of Accounting and Data Analytics team. Our team builds and maintains data platform for sourcing, merging and transforming financial datasets to extract business insights, improve controllership and support financial month-end close periods. As a contributor to a crucial project, you will focus on building scalable data pipelines, optimizations of existing pipelines and operation excellence.
Qualifications-
• 5+ yrs experience as Data Engineer or in a similar role
• Experience with data modeling, data warehousing, and building ETL pipelines
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field.
• Extensive experience working with AWS with a strong understanding of Redshift, EMR, Athena, Aurora, DynamoDB, Kinesis, Lambda, S3, EC2, etc.
• Experience with coding languages like Python/Java/Scala
• Experience in maintaining data warehouse systems and working on large scale data transformation using EMR, Hadoop, Hive, or other Big Data technologies
• Experience mentoring and managing other Data Engineers, ensuring data engineering best practices are being followed
• Experience with hardware provisioning, forecasting hardware usage, and managing to a budget.
• Exposure to large databases, BI applications, data quality and performance tuning

jobs.description:
A Trans Ops Representative at TOC facilitates flow of information between different
stakeholders (Trans Carriers/Hubs/Warehouses) and resolves any potential issues that impacts
customer experience and business continuity. Trans Ops Specialist at TOC works across two
verticals – Inbound and Outbound operations.
Inbound Operations deals with Vendor/Carrier/FC relationship, ensuring that the freight is
picked-up on time and is delivered at FC as per the given appointment. Trans Ops Specialist on
Inbound addresses any potential issues occurring during the lifecycle of pick-up to delivery.
Outbound Operations deals with FC/Carrier/Carrier Hub relationship, ensuring that the truck
leaves the FC in order to delivery customer orders as per promise. Trans Ops Specialist on
Outbound addresses any potential issues occurring during the lifecycle of freight leaving the FC
and reaching customer premises.
A Trans Ops Representative provides timely resolution to the issue in hand by researching and
querying internal tools and by taking real-time decisions. An ideal candidate should be able to
understand the requirements/be able to analyze data and notice trends and be able to drive
Customer Experience without compromising on time. The candidate should have the basic
understanding of Logistics and should be able to communicate clearly in the written and oral
form. Trans Ops Specialist should be able to ideate process improvements and should have the
zeal to drive them to conclusion.

Key job responsibilities
Communication with external customers (Carriers, Vendors/Suppliers) and internal customers
(Retail, Finance, Software Support, Fulfillment Centers)
· Ability to pull data from numerous databases (using Excel, Access, SQL and/or other data
management systems) and to perform ad hoc reporting and analysis as needed is a plus.
· Develop and/or understand performance metrics to assist with driving business results.
· Ability to scope out business and functional requirements for the Amazon technology teams
who create and enhance the software systems and tools are used by TOC.
· Must be able to quickly understand the business impact of the trends and make decisions that
make sense based on available data.
· Must be able to systematically escalate problems or variance in the information and data to the
relevant owners and teams and follow through on the resolutions to ensure they are delivered.
· Work within various time constraints to meet critical business needs, while measuring and
identifying activities performed.
· Excellent communication, both verbal and written as one may be required to create a narrative
outlining weekly findings and the variances to goals, and present these finding in a review
forum.
Providing real-time customer experience by working in 24*7 operating environment.

jobs.description:
千葉県流山市の大規模物流拠点（フルフィルメントセンター/以下FC）にて、ハンズオンで業務遂行が可能なHRビジネスパートナー（HRBP）を募集しています。
HRBPは、担当するFCで働く従業員をカスタマーと考え、FCのリーダーの人事戦略パートナーとして、ビジネス成長に欠かせない組織・人材面の意思決定をサポートする上での重要な役割を担います。
組織開発、人材育成・配置、キャリア形成の機会、組織文化の醸成など、短期・中長期にわたるさまざまなHRソリューションを人事部門の他チームやメンバーと連携・協働しながら提供します。
また、勤務地は埼玉県坂戸市ですが、影響範囲は勤務するFCに留まらず、様々なプロジェクトを通じて他拠点にも貢献する機会があります。

Key job responsibilities
このポジションでは、採用、評価、配置、退職といった一連の人事管理と、FCで働く従業員の勤怠や労務環境を管理する労務管理について、ハンズオンなアプローチで責任を持って業務遂行して頂きます。
具体的には、人事データを分析し、FCの組織における問題点を特定し、タレントマネジメントを行うことで、組織横断的に戦略的な人事配置や人材開発を行なったり、パフォーマンスや言動に問題ある社員対応を行なう等、あらゆる人事課題の問題解決までの方向性を提案、道筋を立ててマネージャーと連携することもあります。
これらの業務を遂行するため、高いコミュニケーション能力とハンズオンのアプローチが必要です。また、論理的かつ分析能力に優れ、労働法、組織開発、報酬・福利厚生等における幅広い知識を持っている必要があります。
FCの従業員構成は、製造業・物流業・小売業等と類似しており、年俸制の社員に加え、時間給パートタイマーや、派遣社員・請負労働者を含みます。
上記業界での経験は、目まぐるしく変化するビジネス環境に適応することに役立ちます。

A day in the life
【HRBPとして働くやりがいとチャレンジ】
事業成長スピードが速い会社の人事として、ビジネス感覚（スピード感）を鍛えることが出来ます。
ヒトをどのように活かしていくのかHR専門性をもって働くことが期待され、責任ある業務がアサインされ、やりがいを感じます。
大きなHR組織でピープルマネジメントするチャンス、グローバル企業ならではの仕事としては、HQから新しい施策を日本国内に導入する機会等、多岐に渡ります。

我々はHRBPのDiversity拡大にも取り組んでいます。HRジェネラリストだけでなく、特定エリアのスペシャリストで、HRBPになることを目指している方も歓迎します。（例: HRコンサルタントやHRのアウトソーシング業務に携わっている方など）。
このようなバックグラウンドの方々には、培ったスキルを発揮し、私たちのHR Operationをより高いレベルに引き上げていただくことを期待しています。

【勤務形態】
・休日：土日祝（カレンダー通り）
・勤務時間：基本9:00～18:00（フレックス制）

About the team
関連情報
・所属部門:人事
・職種:人事

※担当部門:FC
・人事部門の社員インタビューはこちら
・人事職の社員インタビュー
カスタマーサービス(CS)部門/東京オフィス勤務の社員K.I.さん
人事部門/東京オフィス勤務の社員E.N.さん
人事部門/川口FC勤務の社員M.K.さん
